bindings.quest_action: find the compound to treat the blighted soil
bindings.quest_stakes: the entire harvest and the settlement's food supply
bindings.quest_threat: a dormant soil fungus that woke up and killed every crop
bindings.quest_origin: a blight that struck without warning

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. Forgive me, I don't normally bother travelers with my troubles. But fate has left me no other door to knock on.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_origin} destroyed {quest_focus}: weeks of growth blackened and rotting overnight. {target_npc} says it's {quest_threat} that's been dormant for years. The only remedy requires {quest_item}, but it only grows in the deep wilds {location_hint}, where the {enemy_type} have been hunting anything that moves.
dialogueChunks.outro: We did everything right, {player_name}. And the earth itself betrayed us. Please, {quest_action}.

playerResponses.text: Nature can be cruel, but it can be fought. I'll find what you need.
A blight with only one cure? Sounds expensive. What can you offer?
Soil fungus is beyond my expertise. You need an alchemist, not an adventurer.

bindings.quest_action: scout a new ore vein and clear the way for miners
bindings.quest_focus: the collapsed mine that sustained us for generations
bindings.quest_stakes: twelve families who depended on the mine for everything
bindings.quest_threat: the collapse of our primary livelihood
bindings.quest_origin: rotted timbers that gave way from sheer age

dialogueChunks.intro: I've been staring at this cup for an hour, {player_name}. Can't bring myself to drink it because I keep thinking: what if this is the last clean water we have?
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} collapsed last week. Not sabotage, just age: {quest_origin}. {quest_stakes} have nothing now. {target_npc} found traces of a new vein {location_hint}, but the area is thick with {enemy_type} and we'd need {quest_item} to shore up any new excavation. Bad luck, piled on bad luck.
dialogueChunks.outro: Some people would say we're cursed. I'd say we just need someone to {quest_action}.

playerResponses.text: Luck changes. Let me help change yours.
I'll scout the new vein and clear the way. But luck isn't free.
Collapsed mines and dangerous territory? That's a dangerous combination I'd rather avoid.

bindings.quest_action: retrieve the cure ingredients from the marshes
bindings.quest_focus: the settlement where people are quietly wasting away
bindings.quest_stakes: the sick who grow weaker by the day
bindings.quest_threat: a creeping sickness with only one known treatment
bindings.quest_origin: an illness that started with a cough and ends with people too weak to move

dialogueChunks.intro: I should warn you, {player_name}: what I'm about to tell you is the kind of story that makes people walk away. I hope you're different.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} has been creeping through {quest_focus} for weeks. {target_npc} was the first to fall and hasn't left their bed in ten days. The alchemist says the only treatment requires {quest_item} found in the marshes {location_hint}, but the {enemy_type} have claimed the marshes.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not being attacked. We're not at war. We're just slowly, quietly dying. Please, {quest_action}.

playerResponses.text: I care. Show me where these marshes are, and I'll bring back what the alchemist needs.
Marshes crawling with {enemy_type}? I'll go, but it won't be pleasant.
A slow plague in a swamp full of monsters? I value my health too much.

bindings.quest_action: break through the infested paths and reconnect us to the outside world
bindings.quest_focus: the collapsed trade road that was our lifeline
bindings.quest_stakes: a settlement slowly starving from isolation
bindings.quest_threat: the total loss of our connection to the outside world
bindings.quest_origin: a cliff collapse that buried our only trade route

dialogueChunks.intro: You know what the cruelest part is, {player_name}? We were doing well. For the first time in years, we were thriving.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Then {quest_origin} happened. Overnight, {quest_threat} became real: exports worthless, imports gone. {target_npc} tried to blaze a new trail {location_hint}, but the {enemy_type} infest every alternative path. Without {quest_item} from outside, the craftsmen can't work, the alchemist can't brew, and {quest_stakes}.
dialogueChunks.outro: We didn't make an enemy. We didn't provoke anyone. The cliff just fell. Please, {quest_action}.

playerResponses.text: Isolation can be broken. I'll open a way through.
A settlement cut off from trade is a settlement in trouble. I'll help, but at a cost.
Reopening a trade route through monster territory is more than a single job.
